snippet.title: Noah Kahan - The Great Divide (Live on SNL)
snippet.description:
Stream/download The Great Divide: https://noahkahan.lnk.to/thegreatdivide

Get notified about Noah Kahan's "The Great Divide Tour" here: https://noahkahan.com/pages/tour

Follow Noah Kahan:
Linktree: https://linktr.ee/noahkahanmusic
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/noahkahanmusic
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/noahkahanmusic/ 
TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@noahkahanmusic
Twitter: https://x.com/NoahKahan

Lyrics: 
I can’t recall the last time that we talked
About anything but lookin’ out for cops
We got cigarette burns on the same side of our hands
But we ain't friends, we’re just morons
Who broke skin in the same spot
But I’ve never seen you take a turn that wide
And I’m high enough to still care if I die
So I tried to read the thoughts that you worked overtime to stop
You said fuck off, and I said nothing for a while
You know I think about you all the time
And my deep misunderstanding of your life
And how bad it must have been for you back then
And how hard it was to keep it all inside
I hope you settle down, I hope you marry rich
I hope you’re scared of only ordinary shit
Like murderers and ghosts and cancer on your skin
And not your soul, and what he might do with it
You inched yourself across the great divide
While we drove aimlessly along the Twin State line
I heard nothin' but the bass in every ballad that you'd play
While you swore to God the singer read your mind
But the world is scared of hesitatin' things
Yeah, they only shoot the birds who cannot sing
And I'm finally aware of how shitty and unfair
It was to stare ahead like everything was find
You know I think about you all the time
And my deep misunderstanding of your life
And how bad it must have been for you back then
And how hard it was to keep it all inside
I hope you settle down, I hope you marry rich
I hope you’re scared of only ordinary shit
Like murderers and ghosts and cancer on your skin
And not your soul, and what he might do with it
Rage, in small ways
Did you wish that I could know?
You'd fade, to some place
I wasn't brave enough to go
I hope you settle down, I hope you marry rich
I hope you’re scared of only ordinary shit
Like murderers and ghosts and cancer on your skin
And not your soul, and what he might do with it
Aaah, ooh
I hope you threw a brick right into that stained glass
I hope you’re with someone who isn’t scared to ask
I hope that you’re not losing sleep about what’s next
Or about your soul, and what he might do with it

#noahkahan #thegreatdivide

snippet.title: POV: You're Playing the Hardest 2007 Flash Game
snippet.description:
Today we return to the glorious golden age of Flash gaming to investigate Age of War, a perfectly balanced 2007 strategy classic with absolutely no exploits whatsoever. Except, of course, for the bit where we completely break it.

With nothing but questionable tactics, historical absurdity, and a suspicious quantity of tea, we shall guide humanity from cavemen throwing rocks to futuristic death machines deleting entire timelines. What begins as a simple battle of bases soon becomes a deeply British exercise in economic abuse, turret nonsense, unit spam, and making the game regret ever giving us control.

Is Age of War balanced? Probably not. Is it still one of the greatest Flash games ever made? Absolutely. Can we ruin it using strategy so cursed it belongs in a museum? Naturally.

Put the kettle on, prepare the nostalgia, and witness the most civilised act of digital warfare since someone discovered the special attack button.

Age of War is a perfectly balanced game with no exploits.

I review, breakdown and explain The Boys Season 5 Episode 7 Trailer. I discuss the preview which shows Homelander having the V1, Starlight and Mothers Milk fighting with Dogknott and Sheline, Marie and Jordan from Gen V appearing, Soldier Boy’s death, The Deep’s death, and Butcher, Sister Sage, Frenchie, Kimiko and Hughie looking for a different way of defeating Homelander. I also give my theories and predictions on episode 7, the penultimate episode.

snippet.title: Metallica: Live at The Troubadour, West Hollywood, CA - May 24, 1988 (Full Concert)
snippet.description:
Fight Hunger With The Metallica Family. Metallica fights hunger on tour by donating a portion of ticket sales to local food banks and donating leftover catering to reduce food waste. AWMH often sponsors volunteer opportunities for fans at local food banks during tour stops. Join us in the fight against hunger during #MonthOfGiving2026! For more info on All Within My Hands, visit https://www.allwithinmyhands.org.

Filmed on a camcorder at The Troubadour, West Hollywood, California, on May 24, 1988.

Lars Intro 0:00:00
Creeping Death 0:03:00
For Whom the Bell Tolls 0:10:04
Welcome Home (Sanitarium) 0:14:37
The Four Horsemen 0:20:44
Whiplash 0:27:27
Fade to Black 0:31:46
Seek & Destroy 0:38:50
Master of Puppets 0:46:17
Encore Jam 0:57:27
Last Caress 0:58:48
Am I Evil 1:00:10
Battery 1:03:55
London Dungeon Jam 1:11:30
Harvester of Sorrow 1:13:09
Leper Messiah 1:18:55
Encore Jam 1:25:45
Blitzkrieg 1:27:19
**BONUS**
Creeping Death (Live at Alpine Valley, May 27, 1988) 1:31:47

A Note from Spider-Dan: "Rare footage can't always be perfect. Yeah, the camcorder doesn't pan over to stage right enough. Sure, the 'mix' of this soundboard recording may not be consistent. But it's Metallica... at The Troubadour... in 1988. Barely out of the studio after finishing "Justice" and just before going out to give rock fans a massive kick in the ass on the Monsters of Rock tour. It's not often you get a chance to take a glimpse at these moments in time. Have fun with it."
